Paths/Backend Development/Node.js Basics

Node.js Basics

Run JavaScript on the server and build your first backend application.

Node.js lets you run JavaScript outside the browser, making it possible to build servers and CLI tools.

Key Concepts

  • Runtime: Node.js uses the V8 engine (same as Chrome) to execute JavaScript.
  • Modules: Organize code with `import`/`export` (ES Modules) or `require` (CommonJS).
  • npm: The package manager for installing third-party libraries.
  • File System: Read and write files using the built-in `fs` module.
  • HTTP Server: Create a basic web server with the `http` module.

Example

import { createServer } from 'http';

const server = createServer((req, res) => {
  res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });
  res.end(JSON.stringify({ message: 'Hello from Node.js!' }));
});

server.listen(3000, () => {
  console.log('Server running on port 3000');
});
